topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my phone with the casual familiarity of a spam email. It was a post from Mr. Harrison, my notoriously strict history teacher, tagging me in a photo from a school event. My jaw tightened almost imperceptibly. I snatched my phone, my thumb hovering over the 'unfollow' button, a distinct tension coiling in my shoulders. I scrolled through his recent activity—a picture of a well-organized bookshelf, a quote about the Roman Empire. It was all just… too much. I felt a sudden, sharp need to put the device down, the glow of the screen feeling invasive. I quickly minimized the app, the silence of my room suddenly feeling heavy and unwelcome.

[story 2]

I was halfway through my lunch, trying to ignore the buzzing anxiety in my chest, when I saw it. A story update from Ms. Evans, my English professor, featuring a selfie taken during a lecture. And there I was, clearly visible in the background, wearing the exact same slightly wrinkled sweater. A slow, simmering heat began to rise in my neck. I chewed on my sandwich, the sound of the wrapper crinkling loud in the sudden quiet of my internal monologue. Why was he documenting everything? I stared at the screen, my knuckles turning white where I gripped my water bottle. It felt like a deliberate, unnecessary intrusion into my personal space, a performance I had no desire to be part of.

[story 3]

It happened during my late-night study session. I was reviewing notes for the upcoming biology exam, the fluorescent light of my desk lamp casting harsh shadows. Then, a notification pinged. It was Professor Davies. A short video clip of him laughing, captioned vaguely about a recent departmental gathering. A tight knot formed just beneath my sternum. I leaned back in my chair, the cheap plastic groaning under my weight. I didn't move to open it immediately. Instead, I stared at the ceiling, counting the cracks, trying to regulate the rapid, shallow rhythm of my breathing. The sheer audacity of it made my stomach clench with a profound sense of annoyance.
